topic: r7i Instance Family Technical Specifications and Performance Characteristics

  entities:
  
    r7i family|Product
    4th-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors|Product
    Sapphire Rapids|Product
    r6i instances|Product
    r6i instance types|Product
    r5 family|Product
    r7i.12xlarge|Product
    r7i.16xlarge|Product
    r7i.24xlarge|Product
    Intel CPU architecture|Product
  
  proposition: The r7i family is a family of instance types.
    
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|IS_TYPE_OF|family of instance types
      
  proposition: The r7i family is powered by 4th-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|POWERED_BY|4th-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors
  
    entity-attributes:
    
  proposition: 4th-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors are code named Sapphire Rapids.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    4th-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors|CODE_NAMED|Sapphire Rapids

  proposition: The r7i family offers significant improvements over r6i instances.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|IMPROVES_OVER|r6i instances
  
    entity-attributes:

  proposition: The r7i family provides approximately 15% better compute price/performance than comparable r6i instance types.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|COMPARED_TO|r6i instance types
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_PERFORMANCE|approximately 15% better compute price/performance

  proposition: The r7i family provides up to 20% higher memory bandwidth per vCPU than comparable r6i instance types.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|COMPARED_TO|r6i instance types
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_BANDWIDTH|up to 20% higher memory bandwidth per vCPU

  proposition: The r7i instance family has a 2-socket Intel CPU architecture.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|HAS|Intel CPU architecture
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_ARCHITECTURE|2-socket Intel CPU architecture

  proposition: The r7i instance family has a 2-socket Intel CPU architecture similar to the r5 family.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i family|SIMILAR_TO|r5 family
    r7i family|HAS|Intel CPU architecture
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_ARCHITECTURE|2-socket Intel CPU architecture

  proposition: The r7i.12xlarge is an instance type in the r7i family.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i.12xlarge|PART_OF|r7i family
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.12xlarge|IS_TYPE_OF|instance type

  proposition: The r7i.12xlarge and smaller types use a single socket.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.12xlarge|USES|single socket

  proposition: The r7i.12xlarge and smaller types use the system memory owned by that single-socket processor.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.12xlarge|USES|system memory owned by that single-socket processor

  proposition: The r7i.16xlarge is an instance type in the r7i family.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i.16xlarge|PART_OF|r7i family
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.16xlarge|IS_TYPE_OF|instance type

  proposition: The r7i.24xlarge is an instance type in the r7i family.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i.24xlarge|PART_OF|r7i family
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.24xlarge|IS_TYPE_OF|instance type

  proposition: The r7i.16xlarge and r7i.24xlarge types use both sockets and available memory.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.16xlarge|USES|both sockets and available memory
    r7i.24xlarge|USES|both sockets and available memory

  proposition: There is some memory-management overhead required between two physical processors in a 2-socket architecture.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:

  proposition: The performance gains scaling up from a r7i.12xlarge to a r7i.16xlarge or r7i.24xlarge instance type are not as linear as you get scaling up at the smaller sizes.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i.12xlarge|SCALES_TO|r7i.16xlarge
    r7i.12xlarge|SCALES_TO|r7i.24xlarge
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i.12xlarge|HAS_SCALING|not as linear performance gains

  proposition: Performance scales linearly with compute capacity up to r7i.12xlarge in the r7i family.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    r7i.12xlarge|PART_OF|r7i family
  
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_SCALING|linear performance scaling up to r7i.12xlarge

  proposition: The r7i family has enhanced memory management between physical processors in the 2-socket architecture.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_FEATURE|enhanced memory management between physical processors

  proposition: The r7i family has improved performance for memory-intensive graph operations.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
    r7i family|HAS_PERFORMANCE|improved performance for memory-intensive graph operations

  proposition: You can estimate the number of vCPUs needed using the formula vCPUs=(latencyxconcurrency)/2 for all of these instance families.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:

  proposition: In the vCPU estimation formula, latency is measured as the average query latency in seconds.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:

  proposition: In the vCPU estimation formula, concurrency is measured as the target number of queries per second.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
    
    entity-attributes:
